method: Six independent read-only reviewer lenses (product, visual/responsive, accessibility, Python/Streamlit, content/DH-method, security/privacy/FAIR) synthesized and re-verified by the main agent against source, tests, rendered DOM, computed contrast, and provenance hashes.

findings.impact: Internal ticket id 'P003' surfaced in primary user-facing copy; opaque to the non-technical audience.
findings.action: Rewrote to 'until it has been implemented and security-tested'; DOM scan confirms 'p003' no longer in rendered copy.

findings.impact: Unbuilt Guided/Research function described in definitional present tense, inconsistent with the honest future-tense+disclaimer used for the uploader.
findings.action: Prefixed both with 'Will run ...'; preserved the 'documented parameter grid' substring asserted by tests.

findings.impact: Active experiment-map marker rendered teal #116f63 at 2.50:1 on the dark sidebar, below WCAG AA 4.5:1 for 0.72rem text; the current step was the least legible nav element. Original accessibility-report.json marked this area passed.
findings.action: Added sidebar-scoped --delta-teal-on-dark #63b6a6 (6.3:1); the same rule on the white right-column panel is unchanged at 6.04:1. See contrast-proof.json.

findings.impact: --delta-coral and --delta-amber never referenced; --delta-blue referenced only via config.toml literal; the 'four-accent palette' was largely dead code that misleads maintainers.
findings.action: Removed the three unused tokens; documented that teal is the single applied accent and badge hues come from Streamlit's named palette.

findings.impact: The 4-row experiment map was hardcoded twice; the copies could drift.
findings.action: Factored into one _experiment_map_markup() helper (byte-identical output). NOTE: the product question of whether to show the map twice on desktop is a separate IA decision, deferred to the owner.

findings.evidence: src/delta_lemmata/webapp.py disabled buttons 'Add metadata table', 'Continue to parameters'
findings.impact: Two disabled buttons lacked the help= (aria-describedby) that the uploader and run button have, so their disabled reason was not programmatically exposed.
findings.action: Added help= text (two new English registry keys) mirroring the run button pattern.

findings.impact: A dev-only filesystem-watch/auto-rerun flag was the committed default for a frozen shell.
findings.action: Set runOnSave = false.

findings.evidence: pyproject.toml [project].dependencies pydantic; unused in src; pinned by tests/test_supply_chain.py:42
findings.impact: Declared runtime dependency never imported (validation uses jsonschema), enlarging footprint/attack surface.
findings.action: NOT changed. Removal touches the locked SBOM/uv.lock and the clean-clone gate, predates P002 (P001 scaffold), and pydantic is a plausible P003 ingestion-validation dependency. Recommend the owner/Codex decide: remove now or confirm P003 use, with a matching test_supply_chain edit.

findings.evidence: webapp.py purpose card 'Interpretive boundary' + boundary panel 'Method boundary' render byte-identical text
findings.impact: Same sentence under two different heading labels invites a hunt for a non-existent distinction.
findings.action: NOT changed. Removing or repurposing the boundary panel is an information-architecture/product decision reserved for the owner (confirmed present in the live render).

findings.impact: Card/row separators are low-contrast for low-vision users; grouping relies on whitespace.
findings.action: NOT changed. WCAG 1.4.11 does not clearly require decorative separators to reach 3:1, and darkening the global line token would shift the deliberately quiet aesthetic. Flagged as a considered quality trade-off.

findings.evidence: webapp.py purpose title role=heading aria-level=2 vs st.subheader (h3) sections; sidebar h3 before h1
findings.impact: A data value (selected purpose) outranks the workflow-section headings in the a11y outline.
findings.action: NOT changed. The two accessibility-competent reviewers disagreed on whether this is even a defect (no downward level skip; aria-level 2 fills the absent-h2 gap), both rated P3, and demoting to level 3 merely trades the value-rank issue for an h1->h3 skip. Documented rather than churned.

findings.impact: The controlled term 'validated' could imply results-validation.
findings.action: Partially mitigated (removed 'validation gate' from corpus.body); remaining occurrences are low-risk wording.

findings.impact: Asserts SEC-10/11 (no raw-text/path leakage) as present fact in an unreachable shell state.
findings.action: NOT changed; low-severity wording, state is unreachable in the shell. Recommend 'is designed to' phrasing when P012/P014 verify.

findings.impact: Unglossed jargon; safety load carried by the first clause.
findings.action: NOT changed; glossing risks a tutorial wall.

findings.evidence: webapp.py purpose detail shows one purpose at a time
findings.impact: The three purposes are not simultaneously comparable at a glance.
findings.action: NOT changed; acceptable point-of-need design.

findings.impact: Residual label ambiguity resolved by body copy.
findings.action: Not a defect.

findings.impact: Custom accents structurally cannot reach badges; possible tonal mismatch.
findings.action: NOT changed; Streamlit widget semantics, unverifiable without side-by-side render.

findings.impact: report.md #5 says header was 'made opaque' but 0.96 leaves 4% bleed (RGB equals canvas, negligible).
findings.action: NOT changed; cosmetic traceability nit.

findings.impact: A future long step name could overflow at very narrow widths.
findings.action: NOT changed; current labels are short; no overflow observed at 360px.

findings.impact: Labels may wrap on very narrow screens.
findings.action: NOT changed; Playwright audit found no horizontal overflow at 360x800.

findings.evidence: ui_theme.py sticky stHeader; no custom :focus rule
findings.impact: A focused control scrolled beneath the sticky header could have its focus ring occluded (default focus rings are preserved).
findings.action: NOT changed; needs a live focus+scroll session tied to executable workflows (P008/P009).

findings.evidence: workbench.py badge_color: str + webapp.py # type: ignore[arg-type]
findings.impact: A future invalid color value would pass mypy and fail at render.
findings.action: NOT changed; the suppression is currently justified. Recommend a Literal type later.

findings.evidence: test_webapp.py hard-codes rendered copy; test_health.py hard-codes version
findings.impact: Copy edits can break UI tests.
findings.action: NOT changed; respected during this repair by preserving all test-asserted substrings/labels.

findings.evidence: provenance/runs/RUN-20260710-0003.json:21 and RUN-20260710-0004.json:20 top-level config_sha256=7baa8295... matches no file; the per-file input_artifacts hash for .streamlit/config.toml (aac2e17d...) matches exactly (recomputed).
findings.impact: The standalone config_sha256 field has an undefined, unverifiable referent (same stale value in both runs). Not dishonest: the authoritative per-file hash is correct.
findings.action: NOT changed (prior evidence must not be overwritten). Recommend Codex define config_sha256 in run.schema.json or reconcile it. This review's new Run records set config_sha256 to the actual raw config hash.

findings.impact: The primary verification run is not pinned to a commit (schema-valid; clean-clone RUN-0004 does pin a888e7c).
findings.action: NOT changed (must not overwrite). Recommend backfilling a888e7c into RUN-0003.git_commit.

findings.impact: The ticket JSON alone slightly overstates scope relative to linked evidence.
findings.action: NOT changed (ticket acceptance evidence must not be rewritten). Recommend mirroring the qualifier if Codex chooses to touch the ticket.

findings.evidence: AC-04 egress evidence uses a macOS sandbox, not a packet capture; config sets no server.address (defaults 0.0.0.0)
findings.impact: Committed config does not itself pin loopback binding or prove egress closure.
findings.action: P014 responsibility. Not a P002 defect.

verified_clean: XSS/escaping: every unsafe_allow_html value passes through html.escape() or is an int; no user input in P002.
public_health returns an allowlisted dict; _safe_build_id uses re.fullmatch; a path/secret build id collapses to 'invalid'.
Purpose/mode state deterministic across reruns (explicit defaults + None-absorbing fallbacks).
No catalog key drift; every referenced key exists; every key is referenced.
Copy denylist clean: 13 phrases (automated + semantic) absent, including 'p003' after the fix.
Both original screenshot sha256 values match the PNGs on disk; every ID the P002 ticket references resolves to a real record.
Zero external requests: all loaded assets are loopback; Streamlit telemetry off; no runtime AI/analytics/login/remote-storage.
